body.title: [awaiting_validation] Launchers must always reach exec claude — unfailable recovery contract (venv best-effort, no fatal interject)
body.intention: superclaude and rescueclaude ALWAYS reach `exec claude` so CC can start and help rebuild from any state; a missing venv is fixed-if-possible and reported, never fatal; no future edit can interject a step that aborts the launch, and nothing in the launch path can hang.
body.description:
Intent (Akien): superclaude should start no matter what — if nothing's there, start ready to help build it out. rescueclaude in particular may be started under ANY circumstance to recover (incl. rebuilding a months-old igor ecosystem on Windows); it must NOT be possible to interject anything that makes the recovery engine fail. venv missing? try to create it; if not, report once started — but always start.

Two structural hazards, fixed structurally (not piecemeal):
1. set -e/-u abort the launch — in recovery scenarios vars are unset and commands fail. superclaude carries `set -euo pipefail`; drop e+u across the launch path (`set +eu` right before require_api_key, through to exec) so no future edit can interject a fatal. rescueclaude already has no set -e — preserve that and keep new code fatal-free.
2. A hang is a failure too. Keep `pip install -e .` OUT of both launch paths (network install can hang for minutes). Only create the venv SHELL (`python3 -m venv`, offline), time-boxed via `timeout`, with guards-for-the-guards (`command -v python3`/`timeout` — both may be absent on a broken box). The rack server + CC perform the actual rebuild once started. Missing/unbuildable venv -> best-effort stderr recovery banner before exec (never a step that can itself fail or block).

**Affected files:** bin/rescueclaude, bin/superclaude
**Design rules:** none apply (recovery launcher shells; no memory-shape, no persistence, no sqlite). Aligns with rescueclaude's existing CONTRACT header (start even when DB/services/package/repo are gone).
**Scope boundary:** IN — unfailable launch path (set +eu in superclaude), best-effort venv-shell creation + recovery banner in both. OUT — pip install in the launcher (deliberately excluded), de-hardcoding rescueclaude's repo path beyond what the test needs, Windows .ps1/.bat/rescueclaude entry points (gated on the WSL-vs-native question — separate follow-up).
**Test plan:** Adversarial/chaos matrix on a sandboxed COPY of rescueclaude (repo path sed'd to a temp dir so the real .venv is never touched), stub `claude` on PATH, assert STUB_CLAUDE execs (launch reached) in every case: (i) .venv absent + python3 present -> venv shell gets CREATED and claude execs; (ii) .venv absent + python3 stripped from PATH -> claude STILL execs + banner; (iii) repo dir deleted (cd falls back to HOME) -> claude execs; (iv) timeout absent from PATH -> claude execs. superclaude: bash -n clean; assert `set +eu` precedes the launch path; run the bootstrap section against a sandbox to show venv-shell creation + graceful degrade (full tmux exec path is unchanged and validated by relaunch).
**Completion criteria:** rescueclaude passes all four chaos cases (claude execs every time); superclaude has set +eu before the launch path and a guarded, hang-free venv bootstrap; both bash -n clean; committed referencing this ticket.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Unfailable launch contract shipped. rescueclaude: best-effort venv-shell create (no pip install), UU_RESCUE_DIR override for hermetic testing, recovery banner. superclaude: set +eu across launch path + guarded venv bootstrap. PROOF: adversarial chaos matrix on real bin/rescueclaude (stubbed claude, PATH-locked) — claude execs in ALL cases: venv absent→created; python3 stripped→banner+launch; repo dir deleted→HOME fallback+launch; timeout stripped→no-timeout branch+launch. 7/7 assertions pass. superclaude: bash -n clean, set +eu verified before launch path (245<268<338), no executed pip install. Full tmux exec path unchanged (relaunch-validated).
